quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 10 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 10 Quiz 12: Statistics & Data Analysis — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 10: Statistics & Data Analysis — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: A data set: $8, 12, 15, 15, 20, 26$. Find the mean and median. Then each value is multiplied by $2$ and increased by $3$. What are the new mean and median?
questions.explanation: Original: mean $= 96/6 = 16$, median $= (15+15)/2 = 15$. Linear transformation $2x+3$: new mean $= 2(16)+3 = 35$, new median $= 2(15)+3 = 33$.

questions.question: A data set has variance $9$. If each value is multiplied by $4$, what are the new variance and standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Multiplying by $c$ multiplies SD by $|c|$ and variance by $c^2$. Old SD $= 3$. New SD $= 3 \times 4 = 12$. New variance $= 9 \times 16 = 144$.

questions.question: In a normal distribution, 95% of the data falls between 30 and 50. Find $\mu$ and $\sigma$.
A normal distribution has $\mu = 78$ and $\sigma = 6$. What percentage of data is above 90?
Find the area under the standard normal curve between $z = -1.2$ and $z = 0.8$. Use: area left of $z = -1.2$ is $0.1151$, area left of $z = 0.8$ is $0.7881$.
questions.explanation: Area between = $0.7881 - 0.1151 = 0.6730$. About 67.3% of data lies between these z-scores.

questions.question: What $z$-score corresponds to the 70th percentile? Use: $z \approx 0.52$ for area $0.70$.
questions.explanation: 70th percentile means area to the left = $0.70$. From the z-table, $z \approx 0.52$.

questions.question: A 95% confidence interval has a margin of error of $6$ with $n = 100$. What sample size is needed to reduce the margin of error to $2$?
questions.explanation: To divide MOE by $k$, multiply $n$ by $k^2$. $6 \to 2$ is a factor of $3$, so $n = 100 \times 3^2 = 900$.

questions.question: A survey of 625 voters finds 52% support a candidate. Find the margin of error and the 95% confidence interval using $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$.
A regression line passes through $(2, 11)$ and $(6, 23)$. Find the equation and predict $y$ when $x = 10$.
A regression line $\hat{y} = 2x + 4$ is fit to points $(1, 5)$, $(2, 9)$, $(3, 11)$, $(4, 11)$. Find which point has the largest absolute residual.
questions.explanation: Residuals: $5-6=-1$, $9-8=1$, $11-10=1$, $11-12=-1$. All have absolute value $1$. Data alternates above and below the line.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 10: transformations of mean, median, variance, and SD; finding $\mu$ and $\sigma$ from intervals; tail probabilities; area between z-scores; percentiles; sample size and MOE; regression from two points; and residual analysis.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) Linear transformation $ax+b$: new mean/median = $a$(old) + $b$, new SD = $|a|$(old SD), new variance = $a^2$(old variance), (2) From $\mu \pm k\sigma$ interval, $\mu$ is the midpoint, $\sigma$ = half-width$/k$, (3) Area between z-scores = larger cumulative − smaller cumulative.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Multiplicative transformations affect spread measures (SD, variance); additive ones do not, (2) The tail beyond $+2\sigma$ is 2.5%, beyond $+3\sigma$ is 0.15%, (3) To reduce MOE by factor $k$, multiply $n$ by $k^2$, (4) Regression slope = $(y_2-y_1)/(x_2-x_1)$.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 10 fundamentals: (1) Mean = sum/n; weighted mean = sum of (weight × value), (2) $\sigma = \sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2}{n}}$, (3) Empirical Rule: 68-95-99.7, (4) $z = \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}$ and $x = \mu + z\sigma$, (5) $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$, CI = statistic $\pm$ MOE, (6) Residual = $y - \hat{y}$, (7) Multiplying data by $c$ multiplies SD by $|c|$ and variance by $c^2$.
